message:
perf(vdbe): promote Copy into try_execute_hot_opcode hot-path

Follow-up to 64d8ce4d (AddImm promotion). `Opcode::Copy` is the most
frequently emitted register-motion opcode in codegen (71 sites vs 5 for
SCopy), driving SELECT projection, expression propagation, and
materialization staging. Its body is a small loop of
`get_reg(..).clone() + set_reg_fast(..)`, and `p3` is almost always 0
(single-register copy), so the dispatch cost dominates the body cost and
promoting it out of the 190-arm main match yields a clean win.

Also adds a `vdbe_pipeline_execute_copy` microbench parallel to the
existing AddImm-based `vdbe_pipeline_execute`, so the Copy dispatch path
is measurable on its own.

Before/after on the new bench (criterion, p<0.05):

  vdbe_pipeline_execute_copy/256   3.1243 us   ->  2.5219 us  (+23.4% thrpt, p=0.00)
  vdbe_pipeline_execute_copy/1024  12.471 us   ->  9.9372 us  (+24.7% thrpt, p=0.00)

AddImm bench re-run confirms no regression: /256 2.8544 us and /1024
11.365 us, both within noise of the post-AddImm baseline.

Bench: `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cc2_vdbe_perf \
  cargo bench -p fsqlite-vdbe --bench pipeline_stages -- \
  vdbe_pipeline_execute_copy --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 4`

Behaviour is byte-equivalent to the original arm (kept intact; the main
match already carries `#[allow(unreachable_patterns)]`).
`test_copy_register` + fsqlite-vdbe lib unit tests unchanged on the
added-code path. Clippy (lib + benches) clean via rch.

Bead: bd-1dp9.6.2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
